                (try {
                  -- Case: i < j
                  have h₅₆ : i < j := by assumption
                  simp_all [h₅₆]
                  <;>
                  apply measurable_fst
                }) <;>
                (try {
                  -- Case: j < i
                  have h₅₆ : j < i := by assumption
                  simp_all [h₅₆]
                  <;>
                  apply measurable_fst
                }) <;>
                (try {
                  -- Default case
                  simp_all
                  <;>
                  apply measurable_fst
                })
              exact h₅₃
            -- Since all cases are measurable, the entire function is measurable.
            simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
            <;>
            aesop
          -- Each entry is measurable, so the matrix is measurable.
          have h₆ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (wignerMatrixMap' ω) i j) := by
            simp_all [wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply]
            <;>
            aesop
          exact h₆
        exact h₄
      -- The matrix is measurable because each entry is measurable.
      have h₇ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
        -- Use the fact that each entry is measurable to prove that the matrix is measurable.
        apply measurable_of_forall_measurable_matrix _
        intro i j
        exact h₃ i j
      exact h₇
    -- The scalar multiplication is measurable.
    have h₈ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω)) := by
      have h₉ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (wignerMatrixMap' ω)) := h₂
      have h₁₀ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) : WignerSpace n → ℝ) := by
        -- The scalar is a constant, so it is measurable.
        apply measurable_const
      -- The scalar multiplication of a measurable matrix by a measurable scalar is measurable.
      have h₁₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the scalar is measurable and the matrix is measurable.
        have h₁₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) : WignerSpace n → ℝ) := h₁₀
        have h₁₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := h₂
        -- Use the fact that the scalar multiplication is measurable.
        have h₁₄ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω)) := by
          -- Use the fact that the scalar multiplication is measurable.
          apply Measurable.smul
          <;> simp_all [h₁₂, h₁₃]
          <;>
          (try
            {
              apply measurable_const
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              apply h₁₃
            })
        exact h₁₄
      exact h₁₁
    exact h₈
  -- The function `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is the same as the function we just proved is measurable.
  have h₂ : (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • (wignerMatrixMap' ω)) := by
    funext ω
    rfl
  -- Therefore, `wignerMatrixMapScaled` is measurable.
  rw [h₂]
  exact h₁

Lean, the `•` operation is on the right of the matrix, and it is indeed continuous because the topology on matrices is the product topology (or matrix norm topology, equivalent for finite dimensions).

But perhaps we don't need to know about `wignerMatrixMap n` being measurable: we can instead try to directly prove that `wignerMatrixMap'` is measurable and then use scalar multiplication

have h₂ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := wignerMatrixMapMeasurable n
  have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
    -- The scaled matrix map is measurable because it is a scalar multiple of a measurable function.
    -- Specifically, `1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)` is a constant scalar, and scalar multiplication
    -- preserves measurability.
    have h₄ : Measurable (fun M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ => (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • M) := by
      -- Scalar multiplication by a constant is a continuous linear map and hence measurable.
      apply measurable_const_smul
      <;> infer_instance
    -- The composition of measurable functions is measurable.
    have h₅ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMapScaled n) := by
      have h₆ : (wignerMatrixMapScaled n : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) =
          (fun M : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ => (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • M) ∘ (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
        funext ω
        simp [wignerMatrixMapScaled]
        <;>
        simp_all [Matrix.smul_apply]
        <;>
        congr <;> funext i j <;> ring_nf
        <;>
        simp_all [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
        <;>
        field_simp
        <;>
        ring_nf
      rw [h₆]
      exact h₄.comp h₂
    exact h₅
  exact h₃
